per_gap_results.gap_description: Floor plan quality limits PRM/RRT distance estimation accuracy.
per_gap_results.rationale: System hypothesis is more specific, includes quantified target (40% reduction) and references an established methodology (U-Net). Baseline is too abstract.

per_gap_results.gap_description: Optimisation objective does not model human adjacency preferences.
per_gap_results.rationale: Both agents agree and the system hypothesis directly targets the noted gap with a concrete MIQP extension. Strong consensus.

per_gap_results.gap_description: MIQP is intractable for large instances.
per_gap_results.rationale: System hypotheses are strong but need clearer scalability benchmarks and comparison baselines before acceptance.

strengths: High win rate (72%) demonstrates system significantly outperforms baseline
Strong hypothesis specificity with quantified improvement targets
Good agent agreement rate on gap G2 (strong consensus)
weaknesses: Low agent agreement on G1 and G3 indicates uncertainty in best approach
Gap G3 hypothesis needs more rigorous comparison baselines
summary.conclusion: The autonomous reasoning system generates competitive hypotheses that substantially outperform the random baseline, particularly on well-defined alignment gaps. Agreement between agents is moderate, suggesting opportunities to improve the debate resolution mechanism.
